Publications Books Information Bulletins Periodicals Explore By Collections Type Members Lok Sabha Number Ministry Debate Type Question Type Title Committee Name Date Useful Links Confidence Motion No-Confidence Motion Government Bills Go PDL Text of Lok Sabha Debates The official report of the proceedings of Lok Sabha is prepared, published and printed by the Lok Sabha Secretariat. The text of debate of each day sitting from 1st to 17th Lok Sabha is part of this collection. Disclaimer Collection(s) Lok Sabha Debates (Text) Lok Sabha Debates (Uncorrected) Text of Debates (English) Browse × Disclaimer The Lok Sabha Debates are not for publication and are put on Internet solely for purpose of immediate general information in the public interest. Parliamentary copyright: All Parliamentary copyrights are reserved. Individuals are authorised to use the content solely for their individual use, i.e., private study, research, and instructional or educational purposes. Copyright and Use: Permission of the Speaker, Lok Sabha is required for any reproduction from the content of this collection. Suitable acknowledgement is required to be made of the source institution and PDL website. There must be faithful verbatim reproduction, no editing etc. is allowed of the matter for which permission is granted. Users are requested to supply to Editorial Branch, Lok Sabha Secretariat, Parliament House Annexe, two copies of the work in which reproduction from the content of this collection is made with the permission of Speaker, Lok Sabha. Without the written permission of the relevant copyright holder (Lok Sabha Secretariat), no part of these web pages may be published in any medium or transmitted to, or stored in, any publicly accessible website or other form of electronic retrieval system, nor accessed in such a manner as to make them appear part of any third party's website or electronic database or retrieval system. The permission to reproduce from the Debates would afford no protection against any legal proceedings that may arise out of such reproduction. Printed copies of the Lok Sabha Debates are available from Sales Section, Parliament House Annexe, New Delhi. Publications Books Information Bulletins Periodicals Explore By Collections Type Members Lok Sabha Number Ministry Debate Type Question Type Title Committee Name Date Useful Links Confidence Motion No-Confidence Motion Government Bills Go PDL Debate by Titles and Members The digitized debates of Lok Sabha placed under Text of Debates have further been categorized/tagged through detailed metadata to provide specific information by Title / Member / Question Type / Ministry Name / Debate Type / Member Participation, etc. Its two sub collections are Part I and Part II. a) Part I ( Lok Sabha Questions) In this collection, Questions and Answers asked and answered on the floor the House in Lok Sabha can be browsed and searched from the categorized data filtered by Lok Sabha number, session number, date, question type, member’s participation, and ministry wise. b) Part II (Other than Questions Answer) In this collection, the discussions held on floor of the house from 1st Lok Sabha to 17th Lok Sabha can be browsed and searched from the categorized data filtered by Lok Sabha number, session number, date, debate type, member’s participation, and title wise. Collection(s) Part 1 (Questions And Answers) Part 2 (Other than Questions And Answers) PM Speeches Browse × Disclaimer The Lok Sabha Debates are not for publication and are put on Internet solely for purpose of immediate general information in the public interest. Parliamentary copyright: All Parliamentary copyrights are reserved. Individuals are authorised to use the content solely for their individual use, i.e., private study, research, and instructional or educational purposes. Copyright and Use: Permission of the Speaker, Lok Sabha is required for any reproduction from the content of this collection. Suitable acknowledgement is required to be made of the source institution and PDL website. There must be faithful verbatim reproduction, no editing etc. is allowed of the matter for which permission is granted. Users are requested to supply to Editorial Branch, Lok Sabha Secretariat, Parliament House Annexe, two copies of the work in which reproduction from the content of this collection is made with the permission of Speaker, Lok Sabha. Without the written permission of the relevant copyright holder (Lok Sabha Secretariat), no part of these web pages may be published in any medium or transmitted to, or stored in, any publicly accessible website or other form of electronic retrieval system, nor accessed in such a manner as to make them appear part of any third party's website or electronic database or retrieval system. Printed copies of the Lok Sabha Debates are available from Sales Section, Parliament House Annexe, New Delhi. Publications Books Information Bulletins Periodicals Explore By Collections Type Members Lok Sabha Number Ministry Debate Type Question Type Title Committee Name Date Useful Links Confidence Motion No-Confidence Motion Government Bills Go PDL Parliamentary Documents One of the basic postulates of representative democracy is to see that governance is carried out according to the wishes of the people. In this context, Parliamentary control over the Executive assumes greater significance. It is aimed at ensuring that the Government delivers and performs to the best of the ability for public good. The two most significant occasions for review of administration are provided by discussion on the Motion of Thanks on the President Address and the Budget. Along with these, a close and continuous check on governmental activities is exercised by Parliamentary Committees. The other collections provided in this collection are Bulletin Part I, II and Resume of the Work by Lok Sabha. Individuals are authorised to use the content solely for their individual use, i.e., private study, research, and instructional or educational purposes. Copyright and Use: Permission of the Speaker, Lok Sabha is required for any reproduction from the content of this collection. Suitable acknowledgement is required to be made of the source institution and PDL website. There must be faithful verbatim reproduction, no editing etc. is allowed of the matter for which permission is granted. Users are requested to supply to Editorial Branch, Lok Sabha Secretariat, Parliament House Annexe, two copies of the work in which reproduction from the content of this collection is made with the permission of Speaker, Lok Sabha. Publications Books Information Bulletins Periodicals Explore By Collections Type Members Lok Sabha Number Ministry Debate Type Question Type Title Committee Name Date Useful Links Confidence Motion No-Confidence Motion Government Bills Go PDL Presidential Addresses At the commencement of the First Session after each general election to the House of the People, that is, Lok Sabha and at the commencement of the First Session of each year, it is one of the constitutional functions of the President to address both Houses of Parliament assembled together and inform Parliament of the causes of its summon. All the Presidential Addresses from 1950 onwards can be browsed by title and date. ? Go! Publications Books Information Bulletins Periodicals Explore By Collections Type Members Lok Sabha Number Ministry Debate Type Question Type Title Committee Name Date Useful Links Confidence Motion No-Confidence Motion Government Bills Go PDL Budget Speeches The speeches of various Finance Ministers and Railway Ministers are part of this collection. The Railways Budget till 2016 was presented as a separate Budget by the Railways Minister. It merged with the General Budget from the year 2017. ? Go!
